creator: khera@kcilink.com
text:
I installed the kaffe port, and went to do "make package" for easier
installation on some remote machines.  The tar command failed to find
some of the specified *.so files in /usr/local/lib/kaffe:

[root@yertle]# make package
===>  Building package for kaffe-1.0.6
Creating package /usr/ports/packages/All/kaffe-1.0.6.tgz
Registering depends: jpeg-6b libungif-4.1.0b1 png-1.0.8.
Creating gzip'd tar ball in '/usr/ports/packages/All/kaffe-1.0.6.tgz'
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libawt-1.0.6.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libawt.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libmath-1.0.6.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libmath.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libnative-1.0.6.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libnative.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libsecurity-1.0.6.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libsecurity.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libzip-1.0.6.so : No such file or directory
tar: can't add file lib/kaffe/libzip.so : No such file or directory
pkg_create: tar command failed with code 256
*** Error code 1

Those files were not built nor installed, yet are listed in the PLIST
file.  So I did a make "deinstall" and the file /usr/local/bin/kopi
was left behind.  Thus "bin/kopi" is missing from the PLIST file.
Also left behind were the lib/kaffe/*.a files with similar names to
the missing *.so files above.

It seems that parts of Kaffe get built without dynamic lib support.
Looking further into it, it seems that libtool complains about
interlibrary dependencies not being known to work, so backs off to
making static libs for these libraries.

I have libtool-1.3.4 installed.

Fix: Update PLIST to know the *.a library versions instead of the *.so
versions, and add bin/kopi to it as well.

The following patch does it:
How-To-Repeat: 
	
cd /usr/ports/java/kaffe
make install
make package
